name: Sliding Doors
preceding_figure_names: Advanced Hip Twists, Alemena, Closed Hip Twist, Curl, Fan, Open Hip Twist, Opening Out From Reverse Top, Rope Spinning, Spiral, Three Alemanas, Three Threes
notes: Alternative hold: Man can keep L hand below chest level, taking R to R hand hold at end of 3, releasing hold with L hand. Then lead Lady with R hand to pass in front of him to R side, releasing hold at end of 5, allowing R hand to trail along Lady's R arm until it reaches her R shoulder blade on step 6. When following with Lady's Spiral, man lead Lady into her spiral turn by lowering L hand, then immediately gaining normal Fan or Open Position hold at end of last step. When following with Progressive Walks Forward, retain R Shadow hold. Variation w/ Lady's R turn to hip twist position: 2: Cucaracha LF to side, whole foot; 3: weight to RF, LF push floor, L heel lift, L hip up, hip diagonally to R and then rotate to R, collect LF near R ankle and then step forward to Man's R side on 4. 4: LF forward to Man's R side. L arm to side, R hand on Man's chest. Preceding Figures: Advanced Hip Twists, Alemena, Closed Hip Twist, Curl, Fan, Open Hip Twist, Opening Out From Reverse Top, Rope Spinning, Spiral, Three Alemanas, Three Threes. Following Figures: Progressive Walks Forward (in Right Shadow Position, hold 2 or 3 (Kiki Walks)), Spiral.

steps.action: Step forward from Fan Position, slightly release left arm tone
Close Left Foot to Right Foot
comments: Raise L hand in front of Lady's head

steps.action: Cuban Cross (RF behind LF) with 1/8 turn to Right
comments: Extend L arm fwd. Take R to L hand hold (Double hold) at waist level.
Lower L arm to waist level. R arm behind Lady's back.

steps.action: Close Right Foot to Left Foot in RSP
Step to side with slight body turn to Right
comments: Option: Man can do side Cucarachas from step 7 to 12. Footwork: Flat on 8 and 11.

steps.action: Transfer weight to Right Foot; close LF to RF in TP, slight body turn to Left to normal position
comments: Extend arms slightly fwd; retain tone in arms.

steps.action: Close Right Foot to Left Foot, start in Fan Position
